seoTitle: Export Your Full Chat History From Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Request and download a complete archive of your Microsoft Teams chat history through the personal data export portal.
intro: Archiving official records or migrating your communication logs from Teams calls for more than manual copy-paste when you need a complete history. This guide walks through requesting a full compiled export through Microsoft's personal data privacy portal.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar and select My Microsoft Account to open your browser-based info console.

steps.heading: Go to the privacy or export section
steps.body: Navigate to the Privacy tab or the dedicated Teams data export portal.

steps.heading: Select chat history
steps.body: Check the Chat history selection box to isolate text conversation records from other data types like media files.

steps.heading: Submit the export request
steps.body: Click Submit Request to authorize the cloud server to begin compiling your archive.

steps.heading: Download once ready
steps.body: Monitor the export status indicator, and once it changes from Pending to Download, click the button to save the compressed file to your local drive.

faq.question: How long does a chat history export take to process?
faq.answer: The video shows the status moving from Pending to Download but does not specify an exact timeframe, so expect some background processing time.

faq.question: Can I choose to exclude media files from the export?
faq.answer: Yes, the Chat history selection box lets you isolate text records separately from larger media assets.

faq.question: Do I need to be an administrator to export my own chat history?
faq.answer: No, this process is available through your personal Microsoft account for a self-service export.

faq.question: What format does the exported file arrive in?
faq.answer: The export downloads as a compressed file container that you extract to review chronologically ordered message files.
